     The Numbers Business Report

Apollo 18 (2011)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $17,686,929Details
International Box Office $8,830,890
Worldwide Box Office $26,517,819
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $4,893,889
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,516,442
Total $6,410,331
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Officially, Apollo 17, launched December 17th, 1972, was the last manned mission to the moon. But a year later, in December of 1973, two American astronauts were sent on a secret mission to the moon funded by the US Department of Defense. What you are about to see is the actual footage which the astronauts captured on that mission. While NASA denies its authenticity, others say it's the real reason we've never gone back to the moon.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$8,704,271 (49.2% of total gross)
Legs:2.03 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:66.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$5,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,328 opening theaters/3,330 max. theaters, 2.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $26,675,369

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 2nd, 2011 (Wide) by Weinstein Co.
International Releases: March 1st, 2012 (Wide) (Peru)
May 4th, 2012 (Wide) (Colombia)
August 31st, 2012 (Wide) (Venezuela)
Video Release: December 27th, 2011 by Weinstein Co./Anchor Bay
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some disturbing sequences, and language.
(Rating bulletin 2186, 8/24/2011)
Running Time: 87 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Epilogue, Found Footage
Plot point: Conspiracy Theory, Mockumentary, Surprise Twist
Social setting: Alien Invasion, Space Program
Source material: Based on an UNTRUE Story
Subgenre: Isolation Horror
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Dimension Films, Bekmambetov Projects
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Officially, Apollo 17, launched December 17th, 1972, was the last manned mission to the moon. But a year later, in December of 1973, two American astronauts were sent on a secret mission to the moon funded by the US Department of Defense. What you are about to see is the actual footage which the astronauts captured on that mission. While NASA denies its authenticity, others say it's the real reason we've never gone back to the moon.
